moves.note: Hold quality tech, watch for entry

plan: Sell all leveraged ETFs (SOXL, SPAL) to eliminate high risk.
Trim SPY to reduce concentration and free up capital.
Reduce QQQI to a small position, favoring stability.
Add Microsoft (MSFT) as a strong, high-scoring addition.
Keep CrowdStrike (CRWD) but set to 'Watch' for potential re-entry.
This plan prioritizes capital preservation with a cash buffer.
conservative.read: This plan aims for low, steady returns with minimal drawdown. It significantly reduces leverage and concentration risk, building a cash buffer for safety. Expect modest growth, around 1.5% per month, with lower volatility.

plan: Trim SOXL to a minimal position, exit SPAL completely.
Sell SMH to remove a weak semiconductor holding.
Reduce QQQ and SPY to lower overall index exposure.
Add Microsoft (MSFT) as a strong tech play.
Add Energy Select Sector SPDR Fund (XLE) for diversification.
This balanced approach reduces risk while seeking growth.
balanced.read: This plan seeks risk-first growth by trimming leverage and reducing concentration. It blends index exposure with quality tech and adds diversification. Expect moderate returns, around 2.5% per month, with controlled risk.

plan: Increase exposure to leveraged ETFs SOXL and SPAL.
Sell QQQI to exit income and focus on growth.
Trim SPY and QQQ to allow for concentrated bets.
Add to strong tech holding CRWD and add MSFT.
Add MicroStrategy (MSTR) for its high risk/reward setup.
This plan maximizes growth potential with higher risk.
aggressive.read: This aggressive plan maximizes growth by increasing leverage and concentrating in high-conviction names and setups. Expect higher potential returns, around 4% per month, but also significantly higher volatility and drawdown risk due to leverage and concentration. Strict stop discipline is crucial.

plan: Sell all leveraged ETFs and volatile tech/semiconductor holdings.
Exit growth-focused QQQ to prioritize income generation.
Significantly increase QQQI allocation for income.
Add SPY for broad market exposure and income potential.
Add Energy Select Sector SPDR Fund (XLE) for diversification.
This plan focuses on generating steady monthly income.
income.read: This plan prioritizes stable monthly income and capital preservation. It shifts away from growth and leverage towards income-generating assets like QQQI and SPY. Expect lower growth, around 1% per month, with significantly reduced volatility and drawdown risk.
